title: saccharum holcoides var. warmingianum
intro: Saccharum holcoides var. warmingianum, also known as the Chinese wild sugarcane, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to China. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Chinese wild sugarcane include treating digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory problems, as well as having potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic effects. The herbal preparations of Chinese wild sugarcane include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make medicinal products, such as salves and ointments, or consumed as a dietary supplement.
